name: Smart India Hackathon 2026
description: India's largest national-level hackathon organized by the Government of India. Teams solve real problems submitted by ministries and PSUs across education, health, agriculture, and smart cities.

name: HackMIT 2026
description: One of the most prestigious student hackathons hosted by MIT. Open to university students globally. 24-hour event with mentors from top tech companies and a strong focus on impactful innovation.

name: Google Solution Challenge 2026
description: Annual challenge for GDSC members to build solutions addressing the UN's 17 Sustainable Development Goals using Google technologies. Top 100 teams get global recognition and prizes.

name: MLH Global Hack Week — AI Edition
description: A week-long virtual hackathon by Major League Hacking focused on AI and machine learning. Daily workshops, career events, and beginner-friendly tracks make this perfect for first-time hackers.

name: Devfolio Hack Summit 2026
description: A flagship hackathon by Devfolio bringing together student developers across India to build full-stack web applications. Strong Web3 and open-source tracks available.

name: CyberPatriot National Youth Cyber Defense
description: Students defend virtual networks against simulated cyberattacks in this prestigious competition. Excellent for beginners exploring network security, ethical hacking, and system hardening.

name: IEEE Xtreme 20.0
description: A global 24-hour online competitive programming challenge organized by IEEE. Open to all IEEE student members. Tests algorithmic thinking, data structures, and problem-solving under pressure.

name: HackCBS 9.0
description: One of Delhi's largest student-run hackathons, organized by Shaheed Sukhdev College of Business Studies. Multiple problem tracks, sponsor challenges, and travel stipends for outstation participants.

name: ETHIndia 2026
description: Asia's largest Ethereum hackathon. Build decentralized apps, DeFi tools, NFT platforms, and blockchain infrastructure. Huge prize pool with multiple sponsor bounties from leading Web3 companies.

name: Flipkart Grid 8.0
description: Flipkart's national engineering campus challenge. Combines an online quiz with a project round. Winners get Pre-Placement Offers at Flipkart — highly coveted by CS students across India.

name: HackTheBox University CTF 2026
description: A Capture The Flag competition exclusively for university students. Challenges span web exploitation, reverse engineering, forensics, and cryptography. Great for students entering competitive cybersecurity.

name: Flutter Puzzle Hack 2026
description: Google and Flutter's official global hackathon challenging developers to create cross-platform apps using Flutter. Judged on creativity, UI polish, and innovative use of the framework.

name: Microsoft Imagine Cup 2026
description: Microsoft's premier global student technology competition. Build AI-powered solutions addressing real-world problems. Access to Azure credits, mentors, and a path to the World Championship in Seattle.

name: Amazon ML Challenge 2026
description: Amazon's annual machine learning competition for students and early-career engineers. Solve complex ML problems involving NLP, computer vision, and recommendation systems with real Amazon datasets.

name: ACM ICPC Asia Regional 2026
description: The most prestigious competitive programming contest for university students. The Asia Regional qualifies top teams for the ICPC World Finals. Exceptional problem-solving skills required.

name: TreeHacks 2026
description: Stanford University's flagship hackathon open to all students. Focus on high-impact projects addressing climate, health, and education. World-class mentors and workshops over 36 hours.

name: HackGT 12
description: Georgia Tech's large-scale student hackathon welcoming over 1,000 participants. Build anything you dream of in 36 hours with access to sponsor APIs, hardware, and expert mentors.

name: PennApps XXV
description: One of the original college hackathons, hosted by the University of Pennsylvania. A 36-hour event that has launched hundreds of successful startups and student projects since 2009.

name: HackNYU 2026
description: New York University's signature hackathon running simultaneously across three global campuses — New York, Abu Dhabi, and Shanghai. Beginner-friendly with themed tracks each year.

name: TCS CodeVita Season 13
description: TCS's global coding contest open to engineering students worldwide. The top performers earn direct Pre-Placement Offers at Tata Consultancy Services. Tests advanced algorithms and DSA.

name: HackerEarth AI/ML Hackathon
description: A month-long online hackathon focused exclusively on machine learning and AI use cases. Build models for computer vision, NLP, or time-series forecasting using provided real-world datasets.

name: NASA Space Apps Challenge 2026
description: An international hackathon run by NASA where teams use open NASA data to tackle challenges related to Earth, space science, aeronautics, and more. Hosted across 300+ cities worldwide.

name: AngelHack Global Hackathon Series 2026
description: AngelHack connects developers, designers, and entrepreneurs to build startups in 24 hours. The global series runs across 20+ cities. Winners get access to HACKcelerator program and investor networks.

name: Meta Hacker Cup 2026
description: Meta's annual international programming competition spanning multiple rounds from Qualification to Finals. Tests advanced algorithmic problem-solving at the highest level. Open to programmers worldwide.

name: HackUPC 2026
description: One of Europe's largest student hackathons, organized by the Polytechnic University of Catalonia. A 36-hour hackathon with travel reimbursements for international students and world-class sponsors.

name: Junction 2026
description: Europe's leading hackathon experience based in Helsinki, Finland. Junction brings together 1,500 hackers, designers, and developers for 48 hours of intense building with top global companies as partners.

name: MLH Localhost: Cybersecurity
description: A beginner-focused cybersecurity weekend event hosted by Major League Hacking. Learn ethical hacking basics, complete CTF challenges, and earn official MLH certifications. No prior experience needed.

name: Kaggle AI Report Competition 2026
description: Kaggle's flagship annual data science and machine learning challenge. Compete individually or in teams to build the best predictive model on a large real-world dataset. Beginner and expert tracks.

name: Infosys Hackathon: InfyTQ 2026
description: Infosys's campus-focused innovation challenge where students build full-stack solutions to enterprise-grade business problems. Top performers receive internship offers and Infosys Certified Developer badges.

name: Google Summer of Code 2026
description: Google's annual program where students contribute to open-source organizations over 3 months. One of the best opportunities for beginners to get real-world open-source experience and a Google stipend.

name: Global Game Jam 2026
description: The world's largest game creation event. Teams of designers, artists, and developers build a playable game in 48 hours around a surprise theme. Hundreds of local sites across 100+ countries.

name: Capture The Flag — picoCTF 2026
description: Carnegie Mellon University's beginner-friendly CTF competition designed specifically for middle and high school students, though open to all. The best entry point into competitive cybersecurity.

name: Hack The Valley 8
description: University of Toronto Scarborough's flagship hackathon. A 24-hour event that fosters creativity, collaboration, and technical innovation among university students from across Canada and the world.

name: MLH Fellowship Kickoff Hackathon
description: Major League Hacking's fellowship program kickoff event. A remote hackathon open to all students. High performers get invited to join the MLH Fellowship — a 12-week paid remote internship alternative.

name: HackerEarth Frontend Development Challenge
description: A month-long challenge focused on building beautiful, accessible, and performant web interfaces. Participants submit a working project with clean code, responsive design, and documentation.

name: ACM-W Celebration of Women in Computing
description: ACM-W's annual hackathon and conference celebrating women in computing. Open to all genders. Features workshops, mentorship sessions, and a 24-hour hackathon with focus on social good projects.

name: Hack36 — MNNIT Allahabad
description: MNNIT Allahabad's inter-college hackathon bringing together 500+ hackers. Multiple domain tracks including HealthTech, EdTech, FinTech, and Smart Cities. First-time hackers are actively encouraged.

name: Myntra HackerRamp: WeForShe 2026
description: Myntra's campus hackathon focused on fashion technology. Build innovative ML-powered solutions for personalization, visual search, trend forecasting, and sustainable fashion. PPO for winners.

name: OpenAI Developer Day Hackathon 2026
description: An official hackathon coinciding with OpenAI's Developer Day. Build novel applications using the GPT-4, DALL·E, or Whisper APIs. Judged on creativity, technical depth, and real-world impact.

name: StudentHack IX — Manchester
description: The University of Manchester's student-led hackathon, one of the UK's biggest. Open to students across Europe. Beginner-friendly with introductory workshops and a full hardware lab on site.

name: GreatUniHack 2026
description: The University of Manchester's inter-university hackathon bringing students from across UK universities. A beginner-centric 24-hour hack with hardware challenges, workshops, and sponsor prizes.

name: Hack For Good — GitHub & Dev.to
description: A fully online hackathon by GitHub and Dev.to focused on building open-source tools for social good. Projects must be public GitHub repositories. Great for students wanting to build a public portfolio.
